response: public double poly(List<Double> xs, double x) {
// Expand search bounds until we find an interval with a sign change
// Binary search (bisection method) for the zero
Uses the Bisection Method — a reliable numerical root-finding algorithm:
1. Find valid bounds — Start with [-1000, 1000] and expand until poly(left) and poly(right) have opposite signs
2. Bisect — Repeatedly halve the interval, keeping the half where a sign change occurs
3. Converge — Stop when the interval is smaller than epsilon (1e-9), returning the midpoint
notes: Code used without modification. Claude also uses bisection method but starts with larger bounds [-1000, 1000] and returns midpoint of final interval instead of left bound.
